PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kit Pau in Propria Persona. Philip Law (Nephew of Ms. Pau) here to translate.

Mr. Law stated that he is not a certified translator. Ms. Pau stated that she understands English but not much. The Court explained its position/concerns with respect to administrators acting on their own behalf with out assistance from an Attorney. Ms. Pau stated that she knows what she has to do, that at time of death the assets were only stocks which were valued at over $150,000.00 and that today the value is down to $120,000.00. Ms. Pau also stated that her son had no house, that he was single, that he lived with them (the parents), that he supported their living expenses and that she wants the money. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration (subject to review of the order). The Court informed Ms. Pau that she needs to do everything according to the law and recommended that she consult with an attorney. The Court sees that a waiver of bond has been attached to the petition. The issue of bond will be determine upon further review of the petition. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal was set for May 19, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)
